hero.title: Who Should I Hire for a Car Accident Lawyer in Kansas?

author.name: Bradley A. Pistotnik (Trial Lawyer)
author.bio: Brad Pistotnik is the CEO of Bull Attorneys®, P.A. and a Kansas trucking and personal injury lawyer with over 45 years of experience. He focuses on serious injury and truck accident cases involving DOT regulations and commercial vehicle safety standards.

schema.headline: Who Should I Hire for a Car Accident Lawyer in Kansas?
schema.description: Hurt in a Kansas car, truck or business-vehicle accident? Call Bull Attorneys in Wichita or Garden City for a free 24/7 consultation statewide.
schema.articleBody: After a Kansas car accident, hire a law firm that understands motor vehicle statutes, medical injuries, insurance tactics, and the added complexity when a crash involves a truck or commercial motor vehicle. Bull Attorneys explains why experience matters for car versus large-truck collisions, how insurers review medical bills and treatment gaps, what not to say to adjusters after a wreck, and Kansas protections like K.S.A. 60-2801 during the first 14 days after an injury. The firm answers calls 24/7 and handles cases statewide from Wichita and Garden City.

featuredImage.alt: Kansas car accident lawyer helping injury victims after a crash

car.text: Injury representation after a Kansas car crash

truck.text: Serious crashes involving semis & commercial vehicles

intro: When you have a car accident in Kansas, you should hire a law firm with expertise and knowledge in statutory law and regulatory law on motor vehicle crashes, like the lawyers at Brad Pistotnik's Bull Attorneys, P.A. personal injury law firm.
items: Our car accident lawyers are trained to recognize unique laws and regulations that apply to certain motor vehicle crashes. This helps us use the laws and regulations to increase your compensation for your injuries and damages. We look at the Kansas Statutes, administrative regulations, the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations, and the ANSI standards for the Transportation Industry, which provide the industry standard of care for the trucking and transportation industry to follow.
The staff of Bull Attorneys are trained to understand medical injuries from car wrecks so they can help you find a medical specialist to treat your injuries.
Our car accident lawyers will give you personal access to their cell phone numbers so you can reach them.
Our car accident attorneys are cross-trained in car accidents and in understanding the complexities of a crash between a car and a truck or commercial motor vehicle (CMV).
We answer the phone at all hours of the day, seven days a week.

We use state and federal motor vehicle laws and regulations as well as ANSI standards for the Transportation Industry because so many car accidents involve business vehicles with employed or hired drivers who act negligently causing crashes.
sections.closing: You can call Bull Attorneys at 316-684-4400, call Brad Pistotnik on his cell phone at 316-706-5020, or call Tony Atterbury on his cell phone at 316-617-9237.

sections.heading: Who Should I Call for a Car Accident With a Large Truck?
intro: When you have a car wreck with a large truck, it is crucial you only hire highly skilled car accident lawyers who also handle trucking crash claims, like Bradley A. Pistotnik and Bull Attorneys, P.A. This creates an immediate need for all of the following.
You should hire Bull Attorneys due to their extensive experience with car crashes involving large trucks and business vehicles, and their vast resources to build your case properly at 316-684-4400.
items: When necessary, they will obtain intensive crash scene investigation.
They help prove fault of the business that owns the truck and its driver.
You should only hire a car accident attorney with at least 30 years of experience handling car accidents with commercial truck drivers and delivery drivers.
You should hire car accident attorneys who help with finding medical doctors who will treat your injuries regardless of health insurance.
You should hire us because of our huge settlements, and the results speak for themselves.

sections.heading: How Do Insurance Companies Evaluate My Medical Bills After a Car Wreck?
intro: When you have a car accident with a car, van, large truck, or other business delivery vehicle, it is important to know that insurance companies evaluate your medical bills to determine if they are usual and customary, whether there are gaps in treatment, and for necessity.
In car wrecks, you need accident lawyers with expertise and training in understanding how state motor vehicle laws and state and federal regulations make the case value go up or down.
items: Call police to report the crash.
When injured, ask for ambulance transfer to a nearby emergency room.
Document witnesses and take photographs.
Speak about all injuries and describe each different body part in explicit detail no matter how small the injury is.

When your car wreck involves a business vehicle, you should call a respected authoritative car accident attorney who devotes all of their time to car and truck accident law firm like Bull Attorneys, P.A., with highly experienced lawyers who are trained to look for legal and regulatory violations in both car accidents and truck accidents.
Do not give any statements to any insurance adjusters at all, even your own insurance company.

Do not wait to get hospital and medical treatment. Most car insurance companies use a medical bill review system that looks for treatment gaps, and a two-week gap is important. One of the programs used by Allstate is made by ADP. Allstate entered into a class action settlement in 2009 because Allstate failed to correctly calculate medical expenses from automobile injury bills, as evidenced by the case of Strasen v. Allstate.

sections.heading: What Should I Not Say After an Accident?
intro: Immediately following an accident with a car, motorcycle, or large truck, you should not admit fault, and you should not give a tape-recorded statement before you have talked with a car accident lawyer and know how badly you are injured. The reasons are many.
items: Most people do not know how badly they are injured in the first twenty-four hours following a car or truck crash.

K.S.A. 60-2801 prevents an insurance company from settling with a car accident victim in the first 14 days when the accident caused injury and the person is under the care of a doctor, healing arts provider, or under care at a hospital.
When asked to sign a medical release or bodily injury claim release in the first 14 days after your car accident, say no without legal advice from experienced car accident attorneys.

When asked to sign a settlement release in these circumstances, you can get out of it by disavowing the settlement in that 14-day period. You should say no, it is too early to know how badly I am injured.
You should not sign anything unless you call Bull Attorneys first at 316-684-4400 to learn about this Kansas law protecting injury claimants from rapidly moving insurance adjusters.

Many insurance companies have their adjusters under a swift 24-hour contact rule to minimize their losses, so you should not take their calls or answer their texts. This is how they settle quickly with innocent injury victims that do not understand the system.

faqs.question: Who should I hire for a car accident lawyer in Kansas?
faqs.answer: Hire a Kansas car accident lawyer who understands motor vehicle statutes, insurance issues, medical treatment, evidence preservation, and the special laws that apply when a crash involves a business vehicle, delivery driver, commercial motor vehicle, or large truck.

faqs.question: Who should I call after a car accident in Wichita, Kansas?
faqs.answer: You can call Bull Attorneys, P.A. in Wichita at 316-684-4400 for a free consultation. The firm answers calls 24/7 and has offices in Wichita and Garden City.

faqs.question: Who should I call for a car accident with a large truck?
faqs.answer: Call a law firm that handles both car accident and trucking crash claims. Large truck cases may involve the trucking company, the driver, crash scene evidence, driver qualification files, maintenance records, Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations, DOT rules, and transportation-industry safety standards.

faqs.question: How do insurance companies evaluate my medical bills after a car wreck?
faqs.answer: Insurance companies often review whether medical bills are usual and customary, whether treatment was necessary, whether treatment is related to the crash, and whether there were gaps in treatment. This is one reason injured people should get medical care promptly and follow their doctors' instructions.

faqs.question: What should I not say after an accident?
faqs.answer: Do not admit fault, guess about what happened, say you are fine if you are hurt, or give a recorded statement before you understand your injuries and have legal advice. It is safer to report the crash, get medical help, document the scene, and speak with a car accident lawyer before dealing with the insurance company.

faqs.question: Should I give a recorded statement to the insurance adjuster?
faqs.answer: Not before you know how badly you are injured and have talked with a car accident lawyer. Insurance adjusters may ask questions that affect fault, medical causation, or the value of your claim.

faqs.question: How soon should I get medical treatment after a Kansas car accident?
faqs.answer: Get medical care as soon as possible if you are hurt. Ask for an ambulance when needed, go to the emergency room or urgent care, tell medical providers every body part that hurts, and follow up with specialists when symptoms continue.

faqs.question: Does Bull Attorneys handle car accident cases in every Kansas county?
faqs.answer: Yes. Bull Attorneys handles injury accident cases across Kansas from offices in Wichita and Garden City, including car accidents, truck accidents, motorcycle accidents, and business vehicle crashes.

faqs.question: Is the consultation free and available 24/7?
faqs.answer: Yes. Bull Attorneys offers free consultations and answers calls 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, including holidays. You can call the Wichita office at 316-684-4400 or the Garden City office at 620-843-2855.

legalDisclaimer.heading: Legal Advertising Disclaimer
legalDisclaimer.text: This blog provides general information only, not legal advice. Reading it does not create an attorney-client relationship. Every case is different. Results vary depending on each case's facts and legal circumstances. Deadlines and laws can change, so speak directly with an attorney about your accident.
